title: Browser pane — test at mobile / tablet / desktop widths

segments.text: Test your app at any device size — in the dev-env browser pane
segments.subtitle: garrison · a Desktop / Tablet / Mobile selector renders the embedded app at a fixed device width

beats.caption: The browser pane gains a Desktop / Tablet / Mobile viewport selector
beats.expectedScreen: the dev-env split; on the right the browser pane toolbar shows a 3-way Desktop (active) / Tablet / Mobile segmented selector next to DevTools and Refresh, with the app iframe filling the pane (Desktop = fluid)

beats.caption: Mobile — the app rendered at a fixed 390px phone width, framed and centered
beats.expectedScreen: the embedded app iframe shrunk to a narrow ~390px column, centered in the pane with cream gutters on both sides and a rounded device frame

beats.caption: Tablet — a fixed 820px width (it does not fluid-fit the pane)
beats.expectedScreen: the embedded app iframe at a wider ~820px column, still centered in the pane within the device frame

beats.caption: Desktop — fluid again, the app fills the whole pane
beats.expectedScreen: the embedded app iframe back to filling the entire browser pane edge to edge (fluid, no gutters)

segments.text: One pane, every viewport — mobile, tablet, desktop
segments.subtitle: the choice persists across reloads, and works on the mobile Browser tab too
